title: OfflineBanner — No-Connection State Degradation
problem: When a user loses their network connection mid-session, the app currently fails silently — vote buttons appear interactive but fail on submit, session lists appear to work but show stale data with no indication. This creates confusion and distrust.
analysis:
A fixed top banner mounts in AuthShell and listens to `navigator.onLine` + `online`/`offline` browser events. When offline:

- Banner appears: `bg-elevated`, amber left border, `⚡ No connection — showing last loaded data.` text, full-width, fixed below the system status bar area
- Interactive mutation elements (vote buttons, comment submit, stage chip, submit version) become `disabled` with `aria-disabled` and a `title`/`tooltip`: "You're offline"
- Read-only actions (play audio, browse versions, read comments) remain available if data is loaded
- The Recording Screen is an **exception** — recording itself continues offline; the upload banner handles the upload queue

On reconnection (`online` event):
- Banner auto-dismisses with a brief success state: `✓ Back online` (2s) then disappears
- Polling resumes; queued mutations (if any) retry
successCriteria:
- OfflineBanner hidden when navigator.onLine is true
- OfflineBanner appears within 1s of connection loss
- Vote buttons disabled while offline (aria-disabled, tooltip 'You're offline')
- Comment submit disabled while offline
- Stage chip tap blocked while offline
- Submit version button disabled while offline
- Play audio, read sessions/clips/versions, browse comments: unaffected by offline state
- Recording screen: no OfflineBanner; upload queue handles separately
- On reconnect: banner shows 'Back online' for 2s then dismisses
- Banner does not shift page layout (fixed positioning, content has top padding = banner height)
implementation:
TODO: implement after Auth Shell layout is stable.

1. components/ui/OfflineBanner/OfflineBanner.tsx: client component. `useOnlineStatus()` hook (see below). Renders fixed top-0 banner when offline. Uses CSS var `--offline-banner-h` (similar to --upload-banner-h pattern) so sticky headers can offset.
2. hooks/useOnlineStatus.ts: `useState(navigator.onLine)` + window event listeners for `online`/`offline`. Returns `{ isOnline, justReconnected }`.
3. AuthShell.tsx: mount OfflineBanner.
4. Mutation components (VotePanel, CommentComposer, StageSelector, SubmitVersionSheet): consume `useOnlineStatus().isOnline` and apply `disabled` when false.

decisions.decision: Recording screen is exempt from OfflineBanner
decisions.rationale: MediaRecorder is fully local — recording works without a connection. Showing an offline banner on the recording screen would alarm the user mid-take when there's nothing actually wrong with their workflow. Upload queuing (UploadBanner + IndexedDB) handles the connectivity issue for uploads.
decisions.recommendation: OfflineBanner checks `usePathname()` and returns null on /record routes.

decisions.decision: Disable mutations, not navigation, while offline
decisions.rationale: Full app lockdown while offline (preventing navigation) is hostile — the user may be browsing session history or reviewing versions while waiting to reconnect. Only block writes; reads from cached state remain available.
decisions.recommendation: isOnline prop/hook propagated to mutation components only. No router.push() restrictions.

knownLimitations: navigator.onLine is unreliable in some environments (can report online while behind a captive portal) — OfflineBanner is best-effort
Queued mutations are not persisted — if the user navigates away and back while offline, pending votes/comments are lost
